payload.content: Production webhook delivery reliability implementation: (1) Idempotency: Include unique event_id in webhook payload. Receiver must deduplicate using this ID. Store delivered event_ids in Redis with TTL=7d: if redis.exists(event_id): return 200 # already processed. (2) Delivery queue: Use message queue (RabbitMQ, AWS SQS, Redis Streams) for async delivery. Publisher enqueues webhook event; workers consume and POST to subscriber URLs. Decouples event generation from delivery. (3) Retry strategy: Exponential backoff with jitter. Initial retry after 1s, then 2s, 4s, 8s, up to max 3600s. Max retries: 5-7 attempts over ~24h. Code: wait_time = min(base * 2^attempt + random(0, 1000ms), max_wait). Stop retrying on 4xx errors (except 429 rate limit). (4) Dead letter queue (DLQ): After max retries, move failed events to DLQ. Monitor DLQ size with alerts. Manual investigation for DLQ events. Option to replay from DLQ after fixing receiver. (5) HMAC signatures: Sign webhook payload with shared secret. Header: X-Webhook-Signature: sha256=<hmac>. Receiver validates: expected = hmac.new(secret, payload, sha256).hexdigest(); if not hmac.compare_digest(expected, signature): abort(401). Prevents spoofing and tampering. (6) Delivery status tracking: Store delivery attempts in database: {event_id, subscriber_url, attempt_count, last_status, last_error, next_retry_at}. Expose status API for subscribers to query delivery state. (7) Rate limiting: Respect subscriber rate limits. If 429 Retry-After header received, pause deliveries to that subscriber for specified duration. Use token bucket: rate_limiter = RateLimiter(rate=100, per=60). (8) Monitoring: Track delivery success rate (target >99.5%), average delivery latency (target <2s), retry rate, DLQ size. Alert if success rate drops below 95% or DLQ size >100.
payload.summary: Production-grade implementation guide for Webhook Event Delivery Reliability Patterns with Retry and DLQ. Covers architecture design, deployment strategy, and validation methodology for API-Design, Webhook, Reliability stack. Includes benchmarks, configuration examples, rollback procedures, and SLO-based monitoring setup for production environments.

bundle_gene.trigger_text: api-design,webhook,reliability,event-delivery,queue,postgresql cdc sync,kubernetes hpa autoscaling,ebpf network traffic analysis,node.js event loop optimization,docker compose network isolation

payload.summary: Production-grade implementation guide for Webhook Event Delivery Reliability Patterns with Retry and DLQ. Covers architecture design, deployment strategy, and validation methodology for API-Design, Webhook, Reliability stack. Includes benchmarks, configuration examples, rollback procedures, and SLO-based monitoring setup for production environments.
strategy: Step 1: Implement event idempotency with unique event_id stored in Redis (TTL=7d) for deduplication, use async message queue (RabbitMQ, SQS) to decouple event generation from delivery

Step 2: Apply exponential backoff retry strategy with jitter (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s up to 3600s max, 5-7 attempts over 24h), move failed events to dead letter queue after exhausting retries with monitoring alerts

Step 3: Secure webhooks with HMAC-SHA256 signatures using shared secrets, implement rate limiting with token bucket (respect 429 Retry-After), track delivery metrics (success rate >99.5%, latency <2s, DLQ size)
